description: This is a scatter plot illustrating the density of cementite with Pnma and Pbnm crystal structures as a function of Cr and Mn mass content respectively. The x-axis represents the mass content of alloying elements, ranging from 0 to 1, while the y-axis represents density in Kg/m³, ranging from 7000 to 7800 Kg/m³. Diamond markers represent density values of Cr-alloyed cementite, and triangle markers represent density values of Mn-alloyed cementite. Both Cr and Mn additions monotonically reduce cementite density: as Cr content increases from 0 to ~0.22, cementite density decreases from ~7750 Kg/m³ to ~7580 Kg/m³; as Mn content increases from 0 to ~0.95, cementite density drops from ~7700 Kg/m³ to ~7080 Kg/m³. This figure provides experimental basis for the non-linear effect of Cr and Mn on cementite density, which is incorporated into the thermodynamic density prediction model (Model II) for ferritic/low alloy steels to improve prediction accuracy.
